hard.next_step: Joyce mentioned about having an internal discussion with her manager about Zycus, since they do not have any tools in place for automation right now
hard.ais_why: On the 2/11/26 call Joyce Kornfeld voiced interest in AI applications for indirect services in financial services and committed to reviewing the Hackett Group Pinnacle AI webinar; same-day demo covered agentic invoice capture/matching/routing, tail-spend negotiation agents, and Merlin Intake on MS Teams, with Joyce pressing into fraud-detection specifics.

north_star_verdict.headline: The only buyer conversation was the 11 Feb intro call; 145 days later Joyce has never replied to three rep invitations and the deal is cold. Genuine pain and AI curiosity exist, but with a single individual-contributor contact, no budget owner, and 'we're not actively looking right now' still standing, the Feb-2027 date is not credible without a fresh two-way re-engagement.
north_star_verdict.math: Created and Qualified 17 Feb 2026, so 139 days in Qualified with zero stage progression. Last genuine two-way buyer touch 11 Feb 2026 (145 days ago); every touch since is rep-side (Apr 14, May 13, Jun 25 email/call attempts with no logged buyer reply), so the deal is cold on the 60-day rule. Close date 06 Feb 2027 is 215 days away. Reaching close from Qualified requires Formal Evaluation, Shortlisted, Vendor Selected, Negotiation and Contract In Progress in roughly seven months, which is only possible if a two-way buyer conversation restarts inside 14 days, the manager/peer managers Joyce named engage within 30 days, and a budget owner is mapped by Q3 2026.
evidence: Only one buyer call on record (Intro Call, 11 Feb 2026): Joyce Kornfeld confirmed manual, offshore-dependent processes and disparate systems across acquired subsidiaries, but framed it as 'more of an educational presentation' and said 'we're not actively looking right now'.

Three rep-side touches since (Task 14 Apr 2026 'Share the P2P PPT with your manager', Task 13 May 2026 Agentic AI Summit invite, Task 25 Jun 2026 Horizon US invite) are all 'Call Attempted / Email Sent' with no buyer reply logged, so buyer engagement has been dark for 145 days.
Next_Step__c references Joyce's intent to have an internal discussion with her manager since Newrez has no automation tooling, but that discussion has never been confirmed back to the deal team.
Single-thread on Joyce, a sourcing individual contributor with no budget authority; no manager, peer manager, or economic buyer has ever joined a call.
Stage has sat at Qualified for 139 days with no advance to Formal Evaluation, consistent with a slow, self-directed buyer rather than an active evaluation.

metrics.narrative: Joyce Kornfeld articulated real operational pain on the 11 Feb intro call but never quantified it. She stated 'all the repetitive tasks get sent offshore, but they're manual today', confirmed 'visibility is a challenge' across Rhythm's acquired subsidiaries, and said she was 'continually looking at how to bring over their contracts, how to leverage their spend'. When Ed Dlugosz showed automated invoice matching, supplier onboarding and tail-spend negotiation agents she reacted with interest ('That is always a challenge for sure', 'It's fascinating') but committed to no cost, headcount, cycle-time or savings figure. The business case is therefore qualitative only; quantifying the offshore labour spend, manual AP workload and maverick-spend leakage is the work that would convert curiosity into a funded project.
sources: Avoma Intro Call 11 Feb 2026, Joyce Kornfeld: 'all the repetitive tasks get sent offshore... they're manual today'
Avoma Intro Call 11 Feb 2026, Joyce Kornfeld: 'visibility is a challenge' / 'we're continually looking at how to bring over their contracts, how to leverage their spend'
SF Next_Step__c: internal discussion with manager 'since they do not have any tools in place for automation right now'

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has been identified or engaged. Joyce is a sourcing individual contributor who said plainly 'I can't go beyond that right now... promising can't be on that right now', so she does not own budget. She twice pointed to the real authority above her: 'I will definitely share this with my manager and my peer managers' and, per Next_Step__c, an intended internal discussion with her manager about Zycus. That unnamed manager (and the peer managers) is the budget-influencing layer, but no name, title or contact exists in Salesforce or on any call, so the economic buyer cannot be asserted. Mapping and reaching that manager is the single most important qualification move; until then there is no path to a commercial conversation.
sources: Avoma Intro Call 11 Feb 2026, Joyce Kornfeld: 'I will definitely share this with my manager and my peer managers'
Avoma Intro Call 11 Feb 2026, Joyce Kornfeld: 'I can't go beyond that right now'
SF Next_Step__c: internal discussion with her manager about Zycus

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria surface only implicitly from Joyce's questions on the 11 Feb call, and are inferred rather than confirmed by a documented requirement set. She probed user adoption and ease of use (agreeing strongly with the 90%-of-rollouts-fail statistic and recalling her own failed intake project, 'we tried to do something like this before AI technology existed'), services-not-just-goods sourcing ('Does it do that for services as well as goods?'), genuine fraud controls on AP ('does it actually do a fraud check... sequential invoice numbers'), configurability, and legacy-system interoperability ('does Merlin Intake sit on top of legacy systems'). These map to adoption, multi-entity visibility, agentic automation and integration as the buyer's likely evaluation axes, but no formal BRD, RFP or weighted criteria exist yet, so all of it is inferred from one educational session.
sources: Avoma Intro Call 11 Feb 2026, Joyce Kornfeld: 'Does it do that for services as well as goods?'
Avoma Intro Call 11 Feb 2026, Joyce Kornfeld: 'does it actually do a fraud check... sequential invoice numbers and errors on the invoice'
Avoma Intro Call 11 Feb 2026, Joyce Kornfeld: 'does Merlin Intake sit on top of legacy systems'

decision_process.narrative: There is no visible buying process yet. The 11 Feb call was explicitly scoped by Chris Gracias and Ed Dlugosz as educational, not discovery, and Joyce confirmed 'we're not actively looking right now'. The only process signal is her stated intention to share the material internally with her manager and peer managers and to 'talk about options and strategies', which is informal exploration, not a funded evaluation with steps, owners or dates. No approval chain, procurement route, or timeline has been named on any call or in any field. The move that creates process visibility is a working session with Joyce plus her manager to establish whether this becomes a formal initiative and what would trigger one.
sources: Avoma Intro Call 11 Feb 2026 (call framed as educational): 'this is more of an educational presentation today'
Avoma Intro Call 11 Feb 2026, Joyce Kornfeld: 'we're not actively looking right now... we talk about options and strategies'
SF Next_Step__c: internal discussion with manager pending

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are in play, which is correct for a Qualified, pre-evaluation deal. There is no MSA, Order Form, SOW, legal review, InfoSec questionnaire, procurement channel or contract-expiration forcing function on record, and Newrez has not entered any commercial motion. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is not populated and Joyce did not name a renewal date on the unnamed incumbent 'primary vendor', so there is no external clock. Paper visibility only becomes relevant after a business case is built and a decision process is defined; the earliest useful step is learning the incumbent contract's renewal timing to see whether a natural forcing event exists.
sources: SF stage Qualified, no commercial or legal fields populated
Avoma Intro Call 11 Feb 2026, Joyce Kornfeld: 'we have one primary vendor' (renewal timing not stated)

identify_pain.narrative: Joyce owns and clearly articulated three current, felt pains on the 11 Feb call. First, manual offshore-dependent operations: 'all the repetitive tasks get sent offshore, but they're manual today', with no agentic automation in her current tool. Second, fragmented systems across Rhythm's acquisitions: 'we have a lot of companies without any systems' and 'visibility is a challenge', blocking consolidated spend, contract compliance and cross-entity leverage. Third, intake and adoption failure: she was 'stuck' in a meeting on 'how do I raise a request' immediately before this call and recalled past P2P rollouts that failed on adoption, agreeing with the 90%-of-rollouts-fail point. The pain is real and owned by Joyce, but she is the pain-feeler, not the budget owner, so pain alone will not fund a project.
sources: Avoma Intro Call 11 Feb 2026, Joyce Kornfeld: 'all the repetitive tasks get sent offshore... they're manual today'
Avoma Intro Call 11 Feb 2026, Joyce Kornfeld: 'we have a lot of companies without any systems' / 'visibility is a challenge'
Avoma Intro Call 11 Feb 2026, Joyce Kornfeld: 'the meeting I was stuck on was how do I raise a request because we're looking at our intake process right now'

champion.narrative: Joyce Kornfeld is a developing-but-weakening champion. On 11 Feb she engaged warmly, connected the demo to her own history ('we tried to do something like this before AI technology existed'), and committed 'I will definitely share this with my manager and my peer managers'. What has developed is genuine product conviction and internal intent to circulate Zycus. What is missing is decisive: she has no budget authority ('I can't go beyond that right now'), she has not produced the manager or peer-manager introduction she promised, and 145 days later she has not answered any of the three rep follow-ups (14 Apr, 13 May, 25 Jun). Her advocacy is real but entirely passive and now cooling, so she cannot yet carry the deal; converting her intent into an actual scheduled meeting with her manager is the test of whether the champion is real.
sources: Avoma Intro Call 11 Feb 2026, Joyce Kornfeld: 'I will definitely share this with my manager and my peer managers'
Avoma Intro Call 11 Feb 2026, Joyce Kornfeld: 'I can't go beyond that right now'
SF Tasks 14 Apr / 13 May / 25 Jun 2026: rep follow-ups with no logged buyer reply

competition.narrative: There is no active competitive evaluation, but the deal is not truly uncontested. The real alternative is the status quo: Joyce said 'we're not actively looking right now', named an unnamed 'one primary vendor' that lacks any agentic capability ('the version we're on today doesn't have any of that integrated'), and described many acquired subsidiaries running on no system at all. So the dominant threat is inertia and no-decision, not a named rival. Newrez could keep managing manually and offshore indefinitely rather than fund a project. The unnamed incumbent is a low, dormant threat only if it presents an AI/automation upgrade path, which Joyce's enthusiasm for Merlin Intake suggests it has not. The wedge is to make the cost of doing nothing concrete and put the agentic-AI adoption story in front of her manager; identifying that incumbent by name is a discovery gap to close.

competitive_position.summary: There is no active competitor and no RFP; the strongest current threat on this deal is no-decision / status quo, not a rival. Joyce said 'we're not actively looking right now' (11 Feb 2026) and Newrez runs an unnamed 'one primary vendor' with no agentic capability plus many acquired subsidiaries on no system at all, so the risk is that Newrez keeps managing manually and offshore and never funds a project. The unnamed incumbent is a dormant, low threat pending its AI roadmap; no SAP Ariba, Coupa, Ivalua, GEP or other named rival appears in any call, field or task, so none is asserted.

best_practice_check.summary: This is a single-threaded, cold, early-stage deal on real AI-driven pain; the win levers are re-opening a two-way conversation, reaching the budget layer Joyce named, and making the cost of doing nothing concrete.
flags: Break single-thread: Joyce is a sourcing IC with no budget authority. Get to the manager and peer managers she named or the deal cannot progress past Qualified.
Convert AI curiosity to urgency: AIS 8 and Joyce's fraud/adoption/agentic questions show genuine pull. Quantify offshore + manual-AP cost so the manager sees a funded case, not a demo.

Name the incumbent and its renewal clock: the 'one primary vendor' and the no-system subsidiaries define the displacement story and any natural forcing event; identify both before recommending a formal evaluation.

champion_strength.summary: Joyce Kornfeld is a developing champion whose advocacy is real but entirely passive and now cooling. She showed genuine conviction and committed to share Zycus with her manager and peer managers, but has no budget authority, has not produced the promised introduction, and has ignored three rep follow-ups over 145 days. She cannot carry the deal alone; the test is whether she will schedule the manager meeting.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus was positioned AI-first and it landed: Ed led with the Merlin Intake control tower on MS Teams, agentic invoice capture/matching/routing, autonomous tail-spend negotiation and supplier onboarding, and Joyce pushed into specifics (fraud checks, services sourcing, legacy integration). The agentic story is well told; the gap is not positioning but reaching a budget owner who will act on it.

The curiosity is genuine but has not converted to buying intent ('we're not actively looking right now'), so it reads as curious, not hungry.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: One educational intro call (11 Feb) proved genuine AI-driven pain but no buying intent; 145 days later the deal is single-threaded on a sourcing IC with no budget owner, buyer silent, and 'not actively looking' still standing, so the only path forward is a value-led re-open into Joyce's manager.

win_position.text: Win position is low because the deal has never left the education stage: the single buyer conversation was framed as educational, Joyce said 'we're not actively looking right now' (11 Feb), and no budget owner has ever been reached.
The upside is real product-market fit: Joyce confirmed manual-offshore pain and adoption failures and engaged deeply with Merlin Intake and agentic AI, which is why AIS reads 8/Moderate.
The deal is single-threaded on Joyce, an individual contributor with no budget authority ('I can't go beyond that right now'), so there is no one who can fund a project today.
The dominant threat is no-decision, not a rival: Newrez can keep managing manually and offshore indefinitely, and 145 days of unanswered follow-ups suggest that is the current default.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is negative: the buyer has not replied since the 11 Feb call despite outreach on 14 Apr, 13 May and 25 Jun, and the stage has sat at Qualified for 139 days.
No senior stakeholder has joined, no commercial or security topic has opened, and the buyer has named no dates, so the deal is still a generic post-demo state.

customer_commitment.text: Commitment is thin: Joyce's only pledge was to share materials internally, which she has not visibly done, and she asked for no next meeting or Zycus-specific next step.
She did volunteer to review the Hackett Pinnacle AI webinar and spoke of discussing 'options and strategies' internally, a small positive signal of ongoing interest.

deal_risk.text: Highest risk is inertia plus single-thread: no economic buyer, no compelling event, no quantified case, and a champion who has gone quiet for 145 days.
Newrez's own history of failed P2P rollouts is an adoption risk that must be neutralised with the Merlin Intake story to keep the manager engaged.

customer_commitment.evidence: Only committed to share materials internally; no reply to three follow-ups over 145 days; 'not actively looking right now'.

deal_momentum.evidence: No buyer touch since 11 Feb; 139 days in Qualified with no stage movement; only generic education so far.

deal_risk.evidence: Single-thread on a no-budget IC, no economic buyer mapped, no compelling event, status-quo/no-decision is the main threat.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only (manual offshore processes, disparate systems, adoption failures); no cost, headcount, cycle-time or savings figure quantified by the buyer.
